sourceMovie.title: Ghost Rider
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Ghost Rider (2007) as a thriller theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The thriller and action blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Constantine
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Ghost Rider clicked for you, Constantine (2005) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and fantasy, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Hellboy
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Ghost Rider clicked for you, Hellboy (2004) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and fantasy, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Ghost Rider?
faq.answer: Try Constantine, Hellboy, First Blood—each shares DNA with Ghost Rider while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Ghost Rider?
faq.answer: Ghost Rider (2007) is best described as Thriller, Action, Fantasy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Ghost Rider part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Ghost Rider Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
